recipes.title: Pho
recipes.description: Aromatic Vietnamese beef noodle soup with rice noodles, thinly sliced beef, and a clear spiced broth.
steps: Parboil beef bones and brisket for 10 minutes, then drain and rinse to remove impurities.
Char halved onion and ginger over an open flame until blackened on the edges.
Toast star anise, cinnamon, cloves and cardamom in a dry pan until fragrant.
Combine bones, brisket, charred aromatics, toasted spices and water; simmer 3 hours, skimming foam.
Season broth with fish sauce and rock sugar, then strain through a fine sieve.
Soak rice noodles in hot water until tender, then drain.
Place noodles in bowls, top with sliced sirloin and shredded brisket, then ladle boiling broth over to cook the raw beef.
Serve with bean sprouts, Thai basil and lime wedges on the side.

recipes.title: Banh Mi
recipes.description: Vietnamese baguette sandwich filled with pate, cold cuts, pickled vegetables, cilantro and chilli.
steps: Julienne carrot and daikon, then pickle in vinegar, sugar and a pinch of salt for at least 30 minutes.
Slice cucumber into long batons and the jalapeno into thin rings.
Warm the baguettes in the oven for 3 minutes to crisp the crust.
Slice each baguette lengthwise, leaving a hinge, and pull out a little of the soft interior.
Spread mayonnaise on one side and pate on the other, then drizzle with soy sauce.
Layer cha lua, pickled vegetables, cucumber, jalapeno and cilantro inside the bread.
Press the sandwich gently and slice in half before serving.

recipes.title: Banh Xeo
recipes.description: Crispy turmeric rice flour crepes stuffed with shrimp, pork and bean sprouts, served with herbs and lettuce.
steps: Soak mung beans for 2 hours, then steam until tender.
Whisk rice flour, coconut milk, water, turmeric and chopped scallions into a thin batter and rest 30 minutes.
Slice pork belly thinly and stir-fry with a little oil until lightly browned, then add shrimp and cook through.
Heat oil in a hot non-stick pan, ladle in batter and swirl to coat thinly.
Add pork, shrimp, mung beans and bean sprouts to one half, cover for 2 minutes.
Uncover and cook until edges are crisp and golden, then fold the crepe over the filling.
Whisk fish sauce, sugar, lime juice and water with chopped chilli and garlic for nuoc cham.
Serve crepes with lettuce, herbs and dipping sauce; wrap pieces in lettuce to eat.

recipes.title: Bun Cha
recipes.description: Hanoi specialty of grilled pork patties and belly served over rice noodles with herbs and dipping sauce.
steps: Mince pork shoulder and combine with chopped shallots, garlic, 2 tbsp fish sauce, 2 tbsp sugar and pepper; form small patties.
Slice pork belly into thin strips and marinate in remaining fish sauce, sugar, garlic and pepper for 1 hour.
Whisk fish sauce, sugar, lime juice and water with sliced garlic and chillies for the dipping broth.
Pickle thinly sliced carrot and green papaya in the dipping broth for 20 minutes.
Grill patties and belly over high heat until charred and cooked through.
Cook vermicelli until tender, drain and rinse under cold water.
Serve grilled pork floating in warm dipping broth with vermicelli, lettuce and herbs alongside.

recipes.title: Goi Cuon
recipes.description: Fresh Vietnamese summer rolls with shrimp, pork, vermicelli, lettuce and herbs in rice paper.
steps: Simmer pork belly in salted water for 25 minutes, then cool and slice thinly.
Boil shrimp until pink, then peel and split lengthwise.
Cook vermicelli, drain and rinse under cold water.
Soften a rice paper sheet briefly in warm water and lay flat on a damp surface.
Layer lettuce, vermicelli, herbs and pork, then place split shrimp closer to the far edge.
Fold sides in and roll up tightly, keeping shrimp visible through the wrapper.
Whisk hoisin, peanut butter, water and sauteed garlic, top with crushed peanuts and serve as dip.

recipes.title: Cao Lau
recipes.description: Hoi An specialty of thick chewy noodles with five-spice pork, herbs and crispy croutons.
steps: Marinate pork in soy sauce, five-spice, sugar, minced garlic and shallots for 2 hours.
Sear pork until browned, add a cup of water and braise covered for 45 minutes until tender.
Slice the cooked pork thinly and reserve the braising liquid.
Blanch noodles in boiling water for 30 seconds, then drain.
In each bowl, place noodles, blanched bean sprouts, lettuce and herbs.
Top with sliced pork, spoon over a few tablespoons of braising liquid and broken rice crackers.

recipes.title: Bun Bo Hue
recipes.description: Spicy beef and pork noodle soup from Hue with lemongrass, shrimp paste and thick rice noodles.
steps: Parboil beef shank and pork hock for 10 minutes, then drain and rinse.
Simmer meats with smashed lemongrass, halved onion and water for 2 hours, skimming foam.
Dissolve shrimp paste in a little broth, strain and add to the pot.
Heat annatto oil with garlic and chilli flakes, add to the broth for color and heat.
Season broth with fish sauce and sugar, then remove meats and slice.
Cook thick rice noodles until tender and divide between bowls.
Top with sliced beef and pork, ladle hot broth over and garnish with banana blossom and cilantro.

recipes.title: Ca Kho To
recipes.description: Vietnamese caramelised fish braised in a clay pot with fish sauce, palm sugar and black pepper.
steps: Rinse catfish steaks with salt water, then pat dry and marinate with fish sauce, pepper, shallots and garlic for 30 minutes.
Melt palm sugar with 1 tbsp water in a clay pot until it turns deep amber caramel.
Carefully add oil and the marinated fish, turning to coat in caramel.
Pour in coconut water and chillies until fish is half submerged.
Cover and simmer 25 minutes, occasionally spooning sauce over the fish.
Reduce sauce until thick and glossy, then top with scallions and extra pepper before serving.

recipes.title: Banh Cuon
recipes.description: Steamed rice flour rolls filled with seasoned pork and wood ear mushrooms, topped with fried shallots.
steps: Whisk rice flour, tapioca, water and a pinch of salt into a thin batter and rest 1 hour.
Soak wood ear mushrooms, then drain and chop finely.
Saute chopped shallots in oil, add pork and mushrooms and cook until pork is crumbly.
Lightly oil a non-stick pan, ladle a thin layer of batter and cover for 30 seconds until set.
Slide cooked sheet onto an oiled tray, place a spoon of filling on one edge and roll up.
Fry remaining shallots until golden brown for garnish.
Whisk fish sauce, sugar, lime juice and water for dipping sauce.
Serve rolls topped with fried shallots and cilantro alongside bean sprouts and dipping sauce.

recipes.title: Mi Quang
recipes.description: Central Vietnamese turmeric noodle dish with shrimp, pork, peanuts and a small amount of rich broth.
steps: Marinate pork ribs and shrimp separately with turmeric, shallots, garlic and fish sauce for 30 minutes.
Brown ribs in annatto oil, add pork stock and simmer 30 minutes until tender.
Add shrimp and cook 3 minutes until pink, then taste and adjust seasoning.
Blanch noodles briefly in hot water and divide between bowls.
Top noodles with ribs and shrimp, then ladle just enough broth to barely cover the noodles.
Crown with crushed peanuts, fresh herbs and a sesame rice cracker, serve with lime.

recipes.title: Cha Ca
recipes.description: Hanoi turmeric and dill fish, marinated and pan-fried then served with vermicelli and peanuts.
steps: Cut fish into 4cm chunks and marinate with turmeric, grated galangal, yogurt, fish sauce and shrimp paste for 1 hour.
Cook vermicelli, drain and rinse under cold water.
Heat oil in a skillet and pan-fry the marinated fish until golden on both sides.
Add lots of dill and scallion pieces to the pan and toss for 1 minute until wilted but vibrant.
Whisk shrimp paste with sugar, lime and chilli for the dipping sauce.
Serve fish on top of vermicelli with peanuts, more dill and dipping sauce alongside.

recipes.title: Nem Ran
recipes.description: Northern Vietnamese fried spring rolls with pork, mushrooms, glass noodles and crab in rice paper.
steps: Soak glass noodles and wood ear mushrooms separately in warm water for 15 minutes, then drain and chop.
Mix pork, crab, noodles, mushrooms, grated carrot, chopped shallots, eggs, fish sauce and pepper.
Brush a rice paper sheet with water until pliable, then place on a board.
Spoon filling near one edge, fold sides in and roll up tightly.
Heat oil to 160C and fry rolls slowly for 6 minutes until pale, then remove.
Increase heat to 180C and fry again 2 minutes until deep golden and crisp.
Whisk fish sauce, sugar, lime juice, water, garlic and chilli for nuoc cham dipping sauce.

recipes.title: Bun Rieu
recipes.description: Tomato and crab paste noodle soup with tofu, pork, congealed blood and shrimp paste.
steps: Simmer pork bones in water for 1 hour to make a clear stock, skimming foam.
Saute halved tomatoes in annatto oil until softened, then add to the stock.
Mix crab paste, ground pork, eggs and shrimp paste, then drop spoonfuls into simmering broth.
Spoonfuls will float to the top as cooked crab cakes after a few minutes.
Add fried tofu and season broth with fish sauce and sugar.
Cook vermicelli, drain and divide between bowls.
Ladle soup with crab cakes and tofu over noodles, serve with banana blossom and herbs.

recipes.title: Bot Chien
recipes.description: Saigon street snack of pan-fried rice flour cakes with egg, scallions and a soy-vinegar dip.
steps: Whisk rice flour, tapioca starch, water and salt, then steam in a square dish for 30 minutes until firm.
Cool the rice cake fully, then cut into 2cm cubes.
Heat oil in a skillet and pan-fry cubes until crisp and golden on at least two sides.
Push cubes to one side, pour beaten eggs around them and let set briefly.
Sprinkle with chopped scallions and toss everything together until eggs are just cooked.
Whisk soy sauce, vinegar, sugar and chilli sauce; serve with shredded green papaya on the side.

recipes.title: Banh Khot
recipes.description: Mini turmeric and coconut rice cakes from Vung Tau topped with shrimp and served in lettuce.
steps: Whisk rice flour, coconut milk, water, turmeric and chopped scallions; rest 30 minutes.
Steam soaked mung beans until soft.
Heat a banh khot pan, add a drop of oil to each well and a peeled shrimp on top.
Pour batter into each well to nearly the top and add a spoon of mung beans.
Cover for 3 minutes, then uncover and cook another 2 minutes until edges are crisp.
Whisk fish sauce, sugar, lime juice and water with chilli and garlic for nuoc cham.
Serve cakes wrapped in lettuce with herbs, dunked in nuoc cham.

recipes.title: Hu Tieu
recipes.description: Southern Vietnamese clear noodle soup with pork, shrimp, quail eggs and scallions.
steps: Parboil ribs and pork loin briefly, then drain.
Simmer ribs, loin, dried shrimp, daikon and onion in water for 1 hour.
Remove pork loin and slice thinly; continue simmering broth and ribs another 30 minutes.
Boil quail eggs for 5 minutes, then peel.
Season broth with fish sauce and rock sugar, then strain.
Cook hu tieu noodles in boiling water until tender, drain and divide between bowls.
Top noodles with sliced pork, ribs, shrimp, quail eggs and scallions, then ladle hot broth over.
Serve with bean sprouts, garlic chives and lime on the side.
